title: Optimize a slow warehouse query
description: Use the query plan and bytes-scanned to cut scan volume on a columnar store rather than guessing at rewrites.
body: Use when a warehouse query is slow or expensive. Do not blindly add indexes as on an OLTP database — columnar warehouses optimize scans via partitioning and clustering, not B-tree indexes. Actions: read the query plan, find the largest scan, prune unneeded columns, ensure filters hit the partition/cluster key, and pre-aggregate if the same rollup recurs. Validation signals include a plan showing partition pruning and a measured drop in bytes scanned and runtime. Risk boundary: an unbounded scan on a huge table runs up cost every execution; if the query cannot prune to the filtered range, escalate to repartition the table before it runs at production cadence.
